title: Validators, Incremental Crawls, Checkpoints, Resume, and Deletion · Runtime Lens: Deletion Tombstone Policy
summary: Every run downloads everything, a 304 loses prior content, checkpoints duplicate rows after crash, and disappeared pages remain reported as current. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the deletion tombstone policy increment in incremental crawl and recovery ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Distinguish temporary failure, redirect, noindex, permission loss, deletion, and absence from discovery before expiring prior records.
Reprocess ambiguous in-flight work safely and deduplicate output by stable run, page, record, and schema identities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-incremental-checkpoint-cache-2
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-incremental-checkpoint-cache-2.
# Case 7417: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def worked_scraper_incremental_checkpoint_cache_2(known_pages: int = 80, reused_pages: int = 52, refreshed_pages: int = 24, tombstones: int = 4, duplicate_outputs: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "authorizationworked2",
        "url7417",
        "response2dekrow",
        "treeworked27417",
        "frontier7417worked2",
        "report2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if known_pages < 1 or reused_pages < 0 or refreshed_pages < 0 or tombstones < 0:
        return False, known_pages, "incremental-count"
    if reused_pages + refreshed_pages + tombstones != known_pages:
        return False, refreshed_pages, "incremental-reconciliation"
    if duplicate_outputs > 0:
        return False, duplicate_outputs, "resume-duplication"
    if reused_pages < 1:
        return False, reused_pages, "validator-reuse"
    assert reused_pages + refreshed_pages + tombstones == known_pages
    return True, reused_pages, "idempotent-incremental-crawl"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-deletion-tombstone-policy-17qfl5
# Competency: scraper-deletion-tombstone-policy.
# Case 1541: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_deletion_tombstone_policy_17qfl5(known_pages: int = 80, reused_pages: int = 52, refreshed_pages: int = 24, tombstones: int = 4, duplicate_outputs: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_17qfl5 = "17qfl5-5lfq71-1541"
    assert evidence_variant_17qfl5
    evidence_axes_17qfl5 = (
        "authorization17qfl5",
        "url1541",
        "response5lfq71",
        "tree17qfl51541",
        "frontier154117qfl5",
        "report5lfq711541",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_17qfl5)) == 6
    if known_pages < 1 or reused_pages < 0 or refreshed_pages < 0 or tombstones < 0:
        return False, known_pages, "incremental-count"
    if reused_pages + refreshed_pages + tombstones != known_pages:
        return False, refreshed_pages, "incremental-reconciliation"
    if duplicate_outputs > 0:
        return False, duplicate_outputs, "resume-duplication"
    if reused_pages < 1:
        return False, reused_pages, "validator-reuse"
    assert reused_pages + refreshed_pages + tombstones == known_pages
    return True, reused_pages, "idempotent-incremental-crawl"

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Deletion Tombstone Policy, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the deletion tombstone policy defect evidence boundary.

For repair the deletion tombstone policy defect, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Deletion Tombstone Policy model · Theory Deletion Tombsto…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Deletion Tombstone Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 2802 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate validators, 304 reuse, atomic checkpoints, idempotent resume, deletion evidence, and output identity; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish temporary failure, redirect, noindex, permission loss, deletion, and absence from discovery before expiring prior records.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Deletion Tombstone Policy model · Theory Deletion Tombsto….

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish temporary failure, redirect, noindex, permission loss, deletion, and absence from discovery before expiring prior records.
